source_intake_record.raw_question_text: At t= 0,a pulse travelling from left to right at 1 cm/s is some distance from the right end as shown in figure. Determine the next time when the pulse will be at that point again.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the needed initial pulse position / distance from the right end is not specified numerically in text and appears to come from the image
multi_step: to get the next time, the solver likely must read spatial information from the figure and combine it with the given speed (1 cm/s)
the wording 'next time when the pulse will be at that point again' suggests temporal/positional reasoning beyond a single direct lookup
this is a physics wave-motion item, so the solution likely involves at least two linked steps: extract geometry from image, then reason about motion/return time
verifiability: answer is a short, concrete value: '8 s'
multiple-choice options are provided and one option matches the answer_text exactly (D: 8 s)
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, answer_present=true, and the image asset exists

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, answer, and the referenced image all exist, with registry_passed=true and no registry issues.
The sample appears strongly image-dependent, but the image asset is available, so the core semantics are still supported rather than missing.
Verifiability is strong because the answer is a short concrete value and the scoring evidence notes a matching multiple-choice option.
There is some risk from small image dimensions and possible reliance on figure details for exact timing, but this is not enough to reject or demote when the essential assets are intact.
